pg23-p1-s2: The experience of any alcoholic will abundantly confirm this.
pg23-p1: stop. The experience of any alcoholic will abundantly confirm this.
pg23-p2-s1:
These observations would be academic and pointless if our friend never took the first drink, thereby setting the
terrible cycle in motion.
pg23-p2-s2: Therefore, the main problem of the alcoholic centers in his mind, rather than in his body.
pg23-p2-s3:
If
you ask him why he started on that last bender, the chances are he will offer you any one of a hundred alibis.
pg23-p2-s4:
Sometimes
these excuses have a certain plausibility, but none of them really makes sense in the light of the havoc an alcoholic's
drinking bout creates.
pg23-p2-s5:
They sound like the philosophy of the man who, having a headache, beats himself on the head with
a hammer so that he can't feel the ache.
pg23-p2-s6:
If you draw this fallacious reasoning to the attention of an alcoholic, he will
laugh it off, or become irritated and refuse to talk.
pg23-p2:
These observations would be academic and pointless if our friend never took the first drink, thereby setting the
terrible cycle in motion. Therefore, the main problem of the alcoholic centers in his mind, rather than in his body. If
you ask him why he started on that last bender, the chances are he will offer you any one of a hundred alibis. Sometimes
these excuses have a certain plausibility, but none of them really makes sense in the light of the havoc an alcoholic's
drinking bout creates. They sound like the philosophy of the man who, having a headache, beats himself on the head with
a hammer so that he can't feel the ache. If you draw this fallacious reasoning to the attention of an alcoholic, he will
laugh it off, or become irritated and refuse to talk.
pg23-p3-s1: Once in a while he may tell the truth.
pg23-p3-s2:
And the truth, strange to say, is usually that he has no more idea why he took
that first drink than you have.
pg23-p3-s3: Some drinkers have excuses with which they are satisfied part of the time.
pg23-p3-s4:
But in their
hearts they really do not know why they do it.
pg23-p3-s5: Once this malady has a real hold, they are a baffled lot.
pg23-p3-s6:
There is the
obsession that somehow, someday, they will beat the game.
pg23-p3-s7: But they often suspect they are down for the count.
pg23-p3:
Once in a while he may tell the truth. And the truth, strange to say, is usually that he has no more idea why he took
that first drink than you have. Some drinkers have excuses with which they are satisfied part of the time. But in their
hearts they really do not know why they do it. Once this malady has a real hold, they are a baffled lot. There is the
obsession that somehow, someday, they will beat the game. But they often suspect they are down for the count.
pg23-p4-s1: How true this is, few realize.
pg23-p4-s2:
In a vague way their families and friends sense that these drinkers are abnormal, but
everybody hopefully awaits the day when the sufferer will rouse himself from his lethargy and assert his power of will.
pg23-p4:
How true this is, few realize. In a vague way their families and friends sense that these drinkers are abnormal, but
everybody hopefully awaits the day when the sufferer will rouse himself from his lethargy and assert his power of will.
pg23-p5-s1: The tragic truth is that if the man be a real alcoholic, the happy day may not arrive.
pg23-p5: The tragic truth is that if the man be a real alcoholic, the happy day may not arrive. He has lost
